summary:
Based on the mix of advanced physical skills alongside notable deficits particularly evident in expressive language, this six-year-old appears more aligned with early childhood development standards around approximately one-and-a-half to three years of age due primarily to challenges in coherent speech production and understanding complex instructions. \boxed{\text{Development Age} = ~24 months}

The summary concisely captures both critical aspects—advanced fine-motor control versus delayed verbal cognition—and places these findings within an approximate range that reflects overall functional maturity consistent across multiple domains assessed here. Agent
 To summarize only the key clinical points:

A 6-year-old child has mixed abilities suggesting a lower developmental age; strong motor skills indicate at least partial peer-level functioning while struggles with storytelling point to severe language delays comparable to a much younger child, roughly equivalent to a 24-month-old based on current assessments. 

This distills the core observations into concise statements focusing directly on comparative milestone achievements against expected norms for various functions including mobility and communicative prowess.
